46	const (
47		// Working and Staged identifiers refer to the working and staged roots in special circumstances where
48		// we expect to resolve a commit spec, but need working or staged
49		Working = "WORKING"
50		Staged  = "STAGED"
51	
52		CreationBranch = "create"

54		// 1GB.

59		// Keep the most recently materialized commits cached.
60		//
61		// This was bumped from 10,000 due to an operational issue
62		// on idearoom's hosted instance on 2026/02/27.
63		//
64		// The cache we use grows in size up to this maximum.
65		// Falling off the cache size can cause a performance cliff
66		// for applications which run lots of dolt_log queries.
67		//
68		// This can currently be override by the environment variable
69		// `DOLT_COMMIT_CACHE_SIZE`. See below.
70		commitCacheSize int = 1024 * 1024
71	)
72	
73	var ErrMissingDoltDataDir = errors.New("missing dolt data directory")
74	
75	// LocalDirDoltDB stores the db in the current directory
76	var LocalDirDoltDB = "file://./" + dbfactory.DoltDataDir
77	var LocalDirStatsDB = "file://./" + dbfactory.DoltStatsDir
78	
79	// InMemDoltDB stores the DoltDB db in memory and is primarily used for testing
80	var InMemDoltDB = "mem://"
81	
82	var ErrNoRootValAtHash = errors.New("there is no dolt root value at that hash")
83	var ErrCannotDeleteLastBranch = errors.New("cannot delete the last branch")
84	
85	func init() {
86		overrideCommitCacheSizeStr := os.Getenv("DOLT_COMMIT_CACHE_SIZE")
87		if overrideCommitCacheSizeStr != "" {
88			overrideCommitCacheSize, err := strconv.Atoi(overrideCommitCacheSizeStr)
89			if err == nil && overrideCommitCacheSize > 0 {
90				commitCacheSize = overrideCommitCacheSize
91			}
92	
93		}
94	}
95	
96	// TableResolver allows the user of a DoltDB to configure how table names are resolved on roots.
97	// This is useful because the user-backed system table dolt_nonlocal_tables allows table names to resolve to
98	// tables on other refs, but sqle.Database is necessary to resolve those refs.
99	type TableResolver interface {
100		ResolveTableInsensitive(ctx *sql.Context, root RootValue, tblName TableName) (trueTableName TableName, table *Table, found bool, err error)
101		ResolveTable(ctx *sql.Context, root RootValue, tblName TableName) (table *Table, found bool, err error)
102	}
103	
104	type SimpleTableResolver struct{}
105	
106	var _ TableResolver = SimpleTableResolver{}
107	
108	func (t SimpleTableResolver) ResolveTableInsensitive(ctx *sql.Context, root RootValue, tblName TableName) (trueTableName TableName, table *Table, exists bool, err error) {
109		trueTableNameString, exists, err := root.ResolveTableName(ctx, tblName)
110		if err != nil || !exists {
111			return TableName{}, nil, false, err
112		}
113		trueTableName = TableName{
114			Name:   trueTableNameString,
115			Schema: tblName.Schema,
116		}
117		table, exists, err = root.GetTable(ctx, trueTableName)
118		return trueTableName, table, exists, err
119	}
120	
121	func (t SimpleTableResolver) ResolveTable(ctx *sql.Context, root RootValue, tblName TableName) (table *Table, exists bool, err error) {
122		return root.GetTable(ctx, tblName)
123	}
124	
125	// DoltDB wraps access to the underlying noms database and hides some of the details of the underlying storage.
126	type DoltDB struct {
127		db  hooksDatabase
128		vrw types.ValueReadWriter
129		ns  tree.NodeStore
130	
131		// databaseName holds the name of the database for this DoltDB instance. Note that this name may not be
132		// populated for all DoltDB instances. For filesystem based databases, the database name is determined
133		// by looking through the filepath in reverse, finding the first .dolt directory, and then taking the
134		// parent directory as the database name. For non-filesystem based databases, the database name will not
135		// currently be populated.
136		databaseName string
137	
138		// Keep a LRU Cache of materialized commits to speed up future commit resolutions
139		commitCache *lru.Cache[hash.Hash, *OptionalCommit]
140	}
141	
142	// IsWorkingSetRef reports whether |ref| identifies the working set or staging area rather than a commit.
143	func IsWorkingSetRef(ref string) bool {
144		return ref == Working || ref == Staged
145	}
146	
147	// DoltDBFromCS creates a DoltDB from a noms chunks.ChunkStore
148	func DoltDBFromCS(cs chunks.ChunkStore, databaseName string) (*DoltDB, error) {
149		vrw := types.NewValueStore(cs)
150		ns := tree.NewNodeStore(cs)
151		db := datas.NewTypesDatabase(vrw, ns)
152		commitCache, err := lru.New[hash.Hash, *OptionalCommit](commitCacheSize)
153		if err != nil {
154			return nil, err
155		}
156		ret := &DoltDB{
157			db:           hooksDatabase{Database: db},
158			vrw:          vrw,
159			ns:           ns,
160			databaseName: databaseName,
161			commitCache:  commitCache,
162		}
163		ret.db.db = ret
164		return ret, nil
165	}
166	
167	// GetDatabaseName returns the name of the database.
168	// Note: This can return an empty string if the database name is not populated.
169	func (ddb *DoltDB) GetDatabaseName() string {
170		return ddb.databaseName
171	}
172	
173	// ExposeDatabaseFromDoltDB unwraps a DoltDB to a datas.Database.
174	// This method should really only be used in cases where we need to get to the internals of the database, such
175	// as scanning for corruption or migrating data formats. Generally avoid this function.
176	func ExposeDatabaseFromDoltDB(ddb *DoltDB) datas.Database {
177		return ddb.db
178	}
179	
180	// LoadDoltDB will acquire a reference to the underlying noms db.  If the Location is InMemDoltDB then a reference
181	// to a newly created in memory database will be used. If the location is LocalDirDoltDB, the directory must exist or
182	// this returns nil.
183	func LoadDoltDB(ctx context.Context, nbf *types.NomsBinFormat, urlStr string, fs filesys.Filesys) (*DoltDB, error) {
184		return LoadDoltDBWithParams(ctx, nbf, urlStr, fs, nil)
185	}
186	
187	func LoadDoltDBWithParams(ctx context.Context, nbf *types.NomsBinFormat, urlStr string, fs filesys.Filesys, params map[string]interface{}) (*DoltDB, error) {
188		if params == nil {
189			params = make(map[string]any)
190		}
191	
192		if urlStr == LocalDirDoltDB {
193			exists, isDir := fs.Exists(dbfactory.DoltDataDir)
194			if !exists {
195				return nil, ErrMissingDoltDataDir
196			} else if !isDir {
197				return nil, errors.New("file exists where the dolt data directory should be")
198			}
199	
200			absPath, err := fs.Abs(dbfactory.DoltDataDir)
201			if err != nil {
202				return nil, err
203			}
204	
205			urlStr = earl.FileUrlFromPath(filepath.ToSlash(absPath), os.PathSeparator)
206	
207			params[dbfactory.ChunkJournalParam] = struct{}{}
208		}
209	
210		// Pull the database name out of the URL string. For filesystem-based databases (e.g. in-memory or disk-based
211		// filesystem implementations), we can determine the database name by looking at the filesystem path. This
212		// won't work for other storage schemes though.
213		name := findParentDirectory(urlStr, ".dolt")
214	
215		params[dbfactory.DatabaseNameParam] = name
216	
217		db, vrw, ns, err := dbfactory.CreateDB(ctx, nbf, urlStr, params)
218		if err != nil {
219			return nil, err
220		}

content.text: Let me precisely separate struct-field holders from function parameters using an AST-aware pass. I'll use a Go-aware grep tracking `struct {` context.

input.command:
cat > /tmp/fields.py << 'EOF'
import re, glob, os
# find struct field declarations whose type references target patterns
targets = [
 r'\*doltdb\.DoltDB', r'\bdatas\.Database\b', r'\btypes\.ValueReadWriter\b',
 r'\*?types\.ValueStore\b', r'\btree\.NodeStore\b', r'\bchunks\.ChunkStore\b',
 r'\bnbs\.NomsBlockStore\b', r'\bnbs\.GenerationalNBS\b', r'\bnbs\.TableFileStore\b',
 r'\bnbs\.NBSCompressedChunkStore\b', r'\bchunks\.ChunkStoreGarbageCollector\b',
 r'\bchunks\.TableFileStore\b', r'\bhooksDatabase\b',
]
tre = re.compile('|'.join(targets))
for path in glob.glob('**/*.go', recursive=True):
    if path.endswith('_test.go') or '/vendor/' in path: continue
    try:
        lines = open(path, encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore').read().split('\n')
    except: continue
    depth = 0
    struct_stack = []  # list of booleans: is this brace a struct?
    in_struct = 0
    # simpler: track when we enter 'struct {'
    i = 0
    stack = []  # each element True if struct-brace
    for ln, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
        # process struct entry detection before counting
        stripped = line
        # count braces, but detect 'struct {'
        j = 0
        # detect field line: inside struct and matches target and not a func
        if stack and stack[-1] and tre.search(line) and 'func' not in line.split('//')[0]:
            code = line.split('//')[0].rstrip()
            # field: starts with identifier, has type; params in struct won't have trailing comma
            if re.match(r'\s+[A-Za-z_]', line) and not code.endswith(','):
                print(f"{path}:{ln}:{line.strip()}")
        # update brace stack scanning char by char for struct detection
        k = 0
        while k < len(line):
            c = line[k]
            if c == '{':
                # look back to see if 'struct' precedes on this line before this brace
                prefix = line[:k]
                is_struct = bool(re.search(r'\bstruct\s*$', prefix)) or bool(re.search(r'\binterface\s*$', prefix)) and False
                stack.append(bool(re.search(r'\bstruct\s*$', prefix)))
            elif c == '}':
                if stack: stack.pop()
            k += 1
EOF
python3 /tmp/fields.py | sort

I have a complete map. No code changes are needed — this is an audit. Here is the exhaustive inventory.

---

# Audit: production holders retaining `*doltdb.DoltDB` or its storage layers

**Core object:** `*doltdb.DoltDB` (`libraries/doltcore/doltdb/doltdb.go:126`). It is built on three storage layers, any of which is itself a stale-handle risk if retained:
- `db hooksDatabase` → `datas.Database` (`doltdb.go:127`)
- `vrw types.ValueReadWriter` (`doltdb.go:128`)
- `ns tree.NodeStore` (`doltdb.go:129`)

Below those: `datas.database` embeds `*types.ValueStore` and holds the storage-root tracker + node store (`store/datas/database_common.go:37-41`); `ValueStore.cs chunks.ChunkStore` (`store/types/value_store.go:76`); `nodeStore.store chunks.ChunkStore` (`store/prolly/tree/node_store.go:66`). These three are where the root is tracked and the chunk store is actually reached.

**Legend:** ⚠️ = **interface-typed sleeper** — the field never names `DoltDB` (or names only a storage interface), so a grep for the type misses it. These are the ones most likely to be forgotten.

---

## A. The core object and its intra-package value types (`doltcore/doltdb`)
Every value handed out by a `DoltDB` privately re-caches its `vrw`+`ns`, so each is a live storage handle that outlives the call that made it:

- `doltdb.go:127-129` — `DoltDB.{db,vrw,ns}` — the object itself.
- `hooksdatabase.go:26-28` — `hooksDatabase.{Database, db *DoltDB, hooks []CommitHook}` ⚠️ — embeds `datas.Database` (storage) and retains a back-pointer plus **every registered commit hook** (see §I).
- `commit.go:49-50` — `Commit.{vrw,ns}`; `root_val.go:136-137` — `RootValue.{vrw,ns}`; `tag.go:29` — `Tag.vrw`; `durable/table.go:129-130` — `durable.Table.{vrw,ns}`; `durable/index.go:356-357` — `durable.Index.{vrw,ns}`. Each reaches the same storage layers the DoltDB is built on.

## B. The storage layers themselves (`store/…`)
- `store/datas/database_common.go:37-40` — `database.{*ValueStore, rt rootTracker, ns}` — **this `rt` is the storage-root tracker the rework centers on.**
- `store/types/value_store.go:76` — `ValueStore.cs chunks.ChunkStore` ⚠️.
- `store/prolly/tree/node_store.go:66` — `nodeStore.store chunks.ChunkStore` ⚠️.
- `store/datas/pull/puller.go:51-52` — `Puller.{srcChunkStore nbs.NBSCompressedChunkStore, sinkDBCS chunks.ChunkStore}` ⚠️ — retains both DBs' chunk stores for the duration of a clone/push/pull.
- `store/spec/spec.go:155-157` — `Spec.{db,vrw,ns}` (CLI/tooling entry).

## C. Module-global DB factory caches — highest blast radius
- `dbfactory/file.go:88-92` + `singletons map` (`file.go:94`) — `singletonDB.{ddb datas.Database, vrw, ns}` ⚠️ — a **process-global map keyed by path** caching the storage layers; every local DoltDB shares these.
- `dbfactory/git_remote.go:99-102` + `gitRemoteCache` (`git_remote.go:105`) — `gitRemoteCacheEntry.{db,vrw,ns}` ⚠️ — same pattern for git-backed remotes.

## D. Environment layer (`doltcore/env`) — the command side
- `env/environment.go:92` — `DoltEnv.doltDB` (lazily loaded via `loadDBOnce`).
- `env/repo_state.go:60-62` — `DbData.{Ddb, Rsw, Rsr}` — the accessor threaded everywhere; `Rsr`/`Rsw` ⚠️ also reach it.
- `env/multi_repo_env.go:49-50` — `MultiRepoEnv.envs []NamedEnv` — holds many `DoltEnv`s (multi-db CLI/server).
- `env/remotes.go:189-195` — `PushOptions.{SrcDb, DestDb}` (+ `Rsr`/`Rsw`) — push config retaining both DBs.
- `env/grpc_dial_provider.go:67` — `GRPCDialProvider.dEnv *DoltEnv`.
- `cmd/dolt/commands/sqlserver/server.go:83` — `sqlserver` config `DoltEnv *env.DoltEnv`.

## E. SQL provider & engine (`doltcore/sqle`, `cmd/.../engine`)
- `sqle/database_provider.go:68` — `DoltDatabaseProvider.databases map[string]dsess.SqlDatabase` ⚠️ — **the primary live DB registry**; each `SqlDatabase` wraps a `*DoltDB` but the field names only the interface.
- `sqle/database_provider.go:99` — `DoltDatabaseProvider.gitRemotes map[string]*doltdb.DoltDB`.
- `cmd/dolt/commands/engine/sqlengine.go:60` — `SqlEngine.provider *sqle.DoltDatabaseProvider` — engine→provider→all DBs.

## F. `sql.Database` implementations (`doltcore/sqle`)
- `sqle/database.go:77-81` — `Database.{rsr env.RepoStateReader ⚠️, rsw env.RepoStateWriter ⚠️, gs dsess.GlobalStateImpl ⚠️, ddb *doltdb.DoltDB}`. `ReadOnlyDatabase` (`database.go:132`) embeds this.
- `sqle/read_replica_database.go:38-44` — `ReadReplicaDatabase.srcDB *doltdb.DoltDB` (plus embedded `Database`).
- `sqle/user_space_database.go` / `clusterdb/database.go` — return `DbData` reaching a ddb (`user_space_database.go:120`, `clusterdb/database.go:188`).

## G. Session state (`doltcore/sqle/dsess`) — per-connection retention
- `dsess/session.go:49` — `DoltSession.provider DoltDatabaseProvider` ⚠️; `:67` `dbCache *DatabaseCache`; `:68` `dbStates map[string]*DatabaseSessionState`; `:65` `gcSafepointController *gcctx.GCSafepointController`; `:59` `statsProv sql.StatsProvider` ⚠️ (the stats controller, §J).
- `dsess/session_cache.go:64-66` — `DatabaseCache.revisionDbs map[revisionDbCacheKey]SqlDatabase` ⚠️ — caches DBs behind the interface (its own doc-comment *claims* these hold no data handles — they do, via `ddb`).
- `dsess/database_session_state.go:32` — `InitialDbState.{DbData ⚠️, Db sql.Database ⚠️}`; `:60` `DatabaseSessionState.globalState globalstate.GlobalState` ⚠️; `:104` `branchState.dbData env.DbData` ⚠️ (+ `headRoot`, `headCommit`, `workingSet`, `writeSession WriteSession` ⚠️ — all reach `vrw`/`ns`).
- `dsess/globalstate.go:101-103` — `GlobalStateImpl.aiTracker *AutoIncrementTracker` (caches per-table state; no raw storage handle, but tied to a db's lifecycle).
- `dsess/transactions.go:93` — `DoltTransaction.db *doltdb.DoltDB`.

## H. Write path (`doltcore/sqle/writer`)
- `writer/prolly_write_session.go:47-53` — `prollyWriteSession.{tables map[…]*prollyTableWriter, aiTracker globalstate.AutoIncrementTracker ⚠️, workingSet *doltdb.WorkingSet, setter dsess.SessionRootSetter ⚠️}` — table writers hold live prolly mutable maps (ns/vrw) and `setter` writes roots back into the DoltDB.

## I. Commit hooks — retained inside `DoltDB.db.hooks` (§A)
All of these are held by the core object itself and reach *other* DoltDBs:
- `sqle/commit_hooks.go:38` — `PushOnWriteHook.destDb`.
- `sqle/commit_hooks.go:101-114` — `AsyncPushOnWriteHook.destDb` **plus `ch chan PushArg` (buffer 2048)**, where `PushArg.{srcDb,destDb}` (`:102-103`) — a queue holding up to 2048 in-flight DoltDB pairs.
- `sqle/commit_hooks.go:322-335` — `DynamicPushOnWriteHook.{dEnv *env.DoltEnv, syncHook, asyncHook}`.
- `sqle/cluster/commithook.go:47-52` — `commithook.{destDBF func()→*DoltDB, srcDB, destDB}`.
- (`LogHook`, `commit_hooks.go:159`, holds no db — noted as *not* a holder.)

## J. Background services
- `sqle/auto_gc.go:93-102` — `AutoGCController.{hooks map[string]*autoGCCommitHook, workCh chan autoGCWork}`; `:302` `autoGCCommitHook.db *doltdb.DoltDB`; `:136` `autoGCWork.db *doltdb.DoltDB` (in-flight on channel).
- `sqle/statspro/controller.go:70-96` — `StatsController.{pro dsess.DoltDatabaseProvider ⚠️, kv StatsKv ⚠️}`; the `StatsKv` impl `sqle/statspro/stats_kv.go:210-216` — `prollyStats.{ddb *doltdb.DoltDB, m/newM *prolly.MutableMap}` ⚠️ — a **separate stats DoltDB** plus its live prolly maps.
- `sqle/cluster/controller.go:87-113` — `Controller.{remoteSrvDBCache remotesrv.DBCache ⚠️, commithooks []*commithook}` (each commithook per §I).

## K. Remote server / remotesapi — chunk store behind narrow interfaces
- `sqle/remotesrv.go:86-88` — `hooksFiringRemoteSrvStore.{RemoteSrvStore ⚠️ (embedded chunk store), ddb *doltdb.DoltDB}` — built per-`Get` from `datas.ChunkStoreFromDatabase` (`sqle/remotesrv.go:41-74`); `remotesrvStore` itself (`:33`) is stateless.
- `sqle/cluster/remotesrv.go:24-38` — `remotesrvStoreCache` (embeds `DBCache` ⚠️) and `remotesrvStore` (embeds `RemoteSrvStore` ⚠️).
- `utils/remotesrv/cscache.go:33` — `LocalCSCache.dbs map[string]remotesrv.RemoteSrvStore` ⚠️ — standalone remote server's **cache of chunk stores keyed by repo path**; `:76` `SingletonCSCache.s remotesrv.RemoteSrvStore` ⚠️.
- `cmd/dolt/commands/transfer.go:264` — `singletonDBCache.cs remotesrv.RemoteSrvStore` ⚠️ (serves a single db over remotesapi for `dolt admin`/clone).
- Interface definition to keep in mind: `remotesrv/dbcache.go:29-38` — `RemoteSrvStore = chunks.ChunkStore + chunks.TableFileStore`, satisfied by `*nbs.NomsBlockStore` / `*nbs.GenerationalNBS`.

## L. GC / storage-root-swap coordination (`gcctx`, `dprocedures`)
- `doltdb/gcctx/gc_safepoint_controller.go:26-30` — `GCSafepointController.sessions map[GCRootsProvider]*GCSafepointSessionState` ⚠️ — server-lifetime map of sessions (each `GCRootsProvider` is a `DoltSession` reaching all DBs); this is what holds the safepoint while the root is swapped.
- `sqle/dprocedures/dolt_gc.go:76-80` — `killConnectionsSafepointController.doltDB`; `:169-176` — `sessionAwareSafepointController.{controller, callSession *dsess.DoltSession, doltDB}`; `:78/:172` `doltDB *doltdb.DoltDB` — per-GC-run holders that call `PurgeCaches`/swap.
- `cmd/dolt/commands/fsck.go:487-488,675-676` — `fsck` structs holding `*types.ValueStore`, `*nbs.GenerationalNBS`, `tree.NodeStore` (transient CLI).

## M. System tables & table functions (`sqle/dtables`, `sqle/index`) — per-query, but retain during execution
Direct `*doltdb.DoltDB` fields: `dtables/stashes_table.go:36`, `commits_table.go:36`, `commit_ancestors_table.go:33,141`, `commit_diff_table.go:45`, `diff_table.go:65`, `diff_iter.go:395`, `column_diff_table.go:43,171,287`, `unscoped_diff_table.go:48,317`, `schema_conflicts_table.go:36,140`, `table_of_tables_in_conflict.go:31`, `remotes_table.go:43`, `tags_table.go:35`, `log_table.go:39`, `status_table.go:37`, `status_ignored_table.go:37`, `sqle/dolt_schemas_diff_table.go:49`, `sqle/dolt_procedures_diff_table.go:49`, `sqle/dolt_schemas_history_table.go:31,117`, `sqle/dolt_procedures_history_table.go:31,117`, `sqle/temp_table.go:65`.
`db dsess.SqlDatabase` ⚠️ fields: `sqle/tables.go:89`, `dtables/conflicts_tables_prolly.go:93`, `dtables/backups_table.go:31`, `dtables/branch_activity_table.go:34`, `dtables/branches_table.go:54`.
Storage-layer fields: `sqle/index/dolt_index.go:599-600` (`ns`,`vrw`), `dtables/conflicts_tables_prolly.go:144-145` (`vrw`,`ns`).

## N. Ephemeral per-operation storage holders (lower risk, listed for completeness)
These cache `vrw`/`ns` obtained from a per-operation `RootValue` and are torn down when the op ends — they can't survive a swap, but they *are* the same shared instances if inspected mid-op:
- Merge: `merge/merge_rows.go:78-79,163-164,489-490`, `merge/three_way_json_differ.go:32`, `merge/merge_prolly_rows.go:1910`.
- Diff: `diff/table_deltas.go:71-74`.
- Index/exec iterators: `sqle/index/prolly_row_iter.go:28,241`, `prolly_index_iter.go:216`, `index_reader.go:354`, `key_builder.go:103`, `kvexec/lookup_join.go:186`, `kvexec/builder.go:173`, `dtables/prolly_row_conv.go:34`, `dtables/constraint_violations_prolly.go:215`, `dtablefunctions/dolt_preview_merge_conflicts.go:581`.
- Import/table readers: `table/typed/json/reader.go:38`, `jsonl_reader.go:40`, `table/typed/parquet/reader.go:45`, `table/untyped/xlsx/reader.go:41`.

---

### Triage guidance for the rework
1. **Swap-critical singletons** (§C, §E `databases`, §G `DatabaseCache`, §K caches, §L `GCSafepointController`): any storage-lifecycle change must invalidate/rebuild these or ship a stale chunk store to every session.
2. **The interface sleepers (⚠️)** are the ones a type-based grep won't catch — `dsess.SqlDatabase`, `env.RepoStateReader/Writer`, `env.DbData`, `remotesrv.DBCache`/`RemoteSrvStore`, `datas.Database`, `chunks.ChunkStore`, `WriteSession`, `SessionRootSetter`, `AutoIncrementTracker`.
3. **Retained hook queues** (§I `AsyncPushOnWriteHook.ch`, §J `autoGCWork` channel) can hold DoltDB references *past* a swap because they buffer work — drain/quiesce them as part of the lifecycle transition.
4. **The commit-hook list on `DoltDB.db.hooks` itself** (§A/§I) means the core object transitively retains other DoltDBs; re-pointing storage without re-registering hooks leaks the old graph.

### Triage guidance for the rework
1. **Swap-critical singletons** (§C, §E `databases`, §G `DatabaseCache`, §K caches, §L `GCSafepointController`): any storage-lifecycle change must invalidate/rebuild these or ship a stale chunk store to every session.
2. **The interface sleepers (⚠️)** are the ones a type-based grep won't catch — `dsess.SqlDatabase`, `env.RepoStateReader/Writer`, `env.DbData`, `remotesrv.DBCache`/`RemoteSrvStore`, `datas.Database`, `chunks.ChunkStore`, `WriteSession`, `SessionRootSetter`, `AutoIncrementTracker`.
3. **Retained hook queues** (§I `AsyncPushOnWriteHook.ch`, §J `autoGCWork` channel) can hold DoltDB references *past* a swap because they buffer work — drain/quiesce them as part of the lifecycle transition.
4. **The commit-hook list on `DoltDB.db.hooks` itself** (§A/§I) means the core object transitively retains other DoltDBs; re-pointing storage without re-registering hooks leaks the old graph.
